steps.title: Identify favorable and total outcomes
steps.content:
In a probability problem, we count favorable outcomes (what we want) and total outcomes (all possibilities). Here:
  • Total houses on the block: 8
  • Houses with red doors (favorable): 3

steps.title: Apply the probability formula
steps.content:
The probability of selecting a house with a red door is the ratio of houses with red doors to total houses:
$$P(\text{red door}) = \frac{\text{houses with red doors}}{\text{total houses}} = \frac{3}{8}$$
That confirms B is correct.
